sourceText: Never give up, for that is just the place and the time that the tide will turn. Persistence is a monumental force of nature. Wait for your moment.

On the Sovereignty of Persistence: Resilience is time's master. A giant understands the darkest hour often precedes the most monumental breakthrough; by holding your focus and effort when others quit, you position yourself to seize the inevitable shift in circumstances. The tide always turns for those who stay in the water.
The bitterest tears shed over graves are for words left unsaid and deeds left undone. Live with monumental urgency. Act now.

Regret is the only true failure. Seize the present moment as a fleeting, monumental opportunity; by daring to speak your truth and execute your vision today, you eliminate the ache of 'what if.' Don't take your music to the grave.
Truth is the only monumental eloquence. A story told from the heart can destroy a thousand laws. Trust the power of your sincerity.
Empathy is a social technology. By sharing the authentic truth of human experience, you bypass intellect and speak directly to the collective soul. Sincerity is your most persuasive argument.

Enduring the monumental humiliation of the class system and the physical horror of Japanese imprisonment and torture. Facing the heartbreaking division of his nation after thirty years of exile and the constant threat of assassination from both foreign and domestic enemies.

Transcended the status of a fugitive to become the father of a republic, proving that a nation exists as long as its spirit is undefeated. Established a monumental standard for cultural leadership, showing that a giant’s impact is found in their ability to dream for their grandchildren.
My desire is the independent sovereignty of my nation. My second desire is the same. My third desire is the same.

Focus is the enemy of mediocrity. True impact comes from dedicating all your energy to one singular, unwavering goal. By defining your 'one thing' and refusing distractions, you build an unstoppable force.
The only thing I wish for in unlimited quantity is the power of a highly developed culture. Military strength is temporary; culture is monumental and eternal power. Elevate your spirit.
Influence endures longer than control. Profound change comes not from force, but from the soul's beauty. By cultivating your intellect and character, you project an attractive, eternal power.
A beautiful face is not as good as a healthy body; a healthy body is not as good as a righteous heart. Integrity is the monumental core of your existence. Align your center.
